intro_text: As r_c crosses a beach, shivering with the cold, {PRONOUN/r_c/subject} {VERB/r_c/hear/hears} a creaking, rushing sound, like a wave — but out of sync with the ocean.
decline_text: r_c decides to head back early.

success_outcomes.text: r_c leaps forward, dashing to the other side of the beach and up onto the rocks. {PRONOUN/r_c/subject/CAP} {VERB/r_c/make/makes} it in time to watch the beach get lost under a tide of water from inland.

s_c scrambles back the way {PRONOUN/s_c/subject} came and watches the beach disappear under a flood of water. It leaves behind a changed landscape, tons of debris — and a large dead sheep. s_c pounces on the opportunity to scavenge.

s_c shrieks at the strange sound, paws digging into the sand as {PRONOUN/s_c/subject} {VERB/s_c/turn/turns} and {VERB/s_c/run/runs} for home. {PRONOUN/s_c/subject/CAP} {VERB/s_c/are/is} proven right when the beach disappears under a flood of water from inland. {PRONOUN/s_c/poss/CAP} caution saved {PRONOUN/s_c/poss} life.

fail_outcomes.text: r_c is in the middle of the beach when the flood hits {PRONOUN/r_c/object}. Freezing water rushes from inland, sweeping {PRONOUN/r_c/object} straight out to sea to die in the cold ocean.

history_text.death: m_c was swept out to sea by a flood.

intro_text: Trekking near a copse of trees where the waves swish at their paws, the patrol notices a huge blue crab picking at a coconut.
decline_text: Deciding that it looks too dangerous to approach, the patrol leaves the crab alone.

success_outcomes.text: The patrol watches as the crab hauls a coconut up the tree, only to drop it with a heavy crack before scrambling down to retrieve it and start again. The strange routine leaves the cats puzzled, but they can't help admiring its determination.

The crab is huge, but surely it can't be that dangerous. However, when p_l describes a story {PRONOUN/p_l/subject} once heard about this giant creature killing a fully-grown cat, the group decides to steer clear. Maybe it's best to continue on with their patrol instead.

Watching the crab attempt to crack open a coconut, the patrol proposes a plan that's inspired by its technique. While they don't have scuttling legs or strong pinchers, the cats are able to gather rocks into a palm tree and send them crashing down onto the coconut below. A sharp crack of the shell indicates their success, and they happily feast on the fruity, fleshy innards.

log.cats_from: cat_from had fun cracking open a coconut with cat_to.

fail_outcomes.text: r_c ventures too near, and the crab's massive claw snaps shut just short of {PRONOUN/r_c/poss} face. Startled, {PRONOUN/r_c/subject} {VERB/r_c/leap/leaps} back into the sand, fur bristling and claws unsheathed. That was far too close! Maybe this is one border threat better left alone.
r_c approaches the crab, but it lashes out with terrifying speed. Its crushing pincers clamp down, leaving {PRONOUN/r_c/object} badly injured before {PRONOUN/r_c/subject} can fully retreat.

history_text.scar: m_c's bone was broken by a coconut crab.
history_text.death: m_c couldn't recover from {PRONOUN/m_c/poss} broken bone after being pinched by a coconut crab.

fail_outcomes.text: r_c lunges to drive the crab off, but its pincers strike {PRONOUN/r_c/object} with a brutal force {PRONOUN/r_c/subject} can't escape from. The patrol can only watch in horror as the attack proves fatal.

history_text.death: m_c was pinched to death by a coconut crab.
